Farn Polrrrcnr. PRACrrcEs CouurssroN
428 J Street o Suite 620 e Sacramento, CA 9581.4-2329
(916) 322-5660 o Fax (916) 322-0886
October 24,2005
Stephanie Mizuno
Assistant City Clerk, City of Sacramento
Historic City Hall, FirSt Floor
915 I Street
S acramento, California 9 581 4
Re: Your Request for Informal Assistance
Our FiIe No. I-05-200
DearMs. Mizuno
This letter is in response tb your request for adviee regarding the campaign provisions of
the Political Reform Act (the "Act').r Because your questions are general in nature, we are
treating your request as one for informal assistance.2
The City of Sacramento is holding a June 6,2006, election. The city's rules require
candidates and committees to file a post-election statement no later than July 5, 2006. The
period covered by the city's post-election statement is May 21,2006 through June 30, 2006,
which is the same period covered by the semi-annual statement required by the Act. The semi-
annual statement is due no later than July 31,2006. You requested approval to combine the
city's post-election statement and the semi-annual statement so that candidates and committees
are not required to file two statements covering the same period.
Nothing in the Act prohibits a local jurisdiction from adopting its own rules imposing
additional requirements provided those rules do not conflict with the Act. (Section 81013.) In
addition, a local jurisdiction may enact its own campaign reporting rules as long as those rules
are imposed only on candidates and committees active in the jurisdiction and do not prevent
I Government Code sections 8 1000 - 91014. Commission regulations appear at Title 2, sections I 8 I 09-
18997, of the Califomia Code of Regulations.
2 Informal assistance does not provide the requestor with the immunity provided by an opinion or formal
written advice. (Section 83 I 1 4; regulation 1 83 29(c)(3).)


File No. I-05-200
Page No. 2
those candidates and committees from complying with the Act's reporting requirements.
(Section 81009.5.) According to regulation l8426,whenever a person is requlred to file a semi-
annual campaign statement, that person may file at any time prior to the date as long as all
expenditures made and contributions received are included. Since the city,s post-election
statement is due prior to the semi-annual statement filing deadline, the semi-annual statement
filing requirement is also satisfied. (Section s4200.) Therefore, the semi-annual filing
requirement will be met by the staternent required to be filed under the city,s rules by July 5,
2006- However, please note that the deadline imposed by the Act for filing the semi-annual
statement remains in effect. Therefore, a later filing would not violate the Act,s filing deadline if
accomplished by July 31, 2006. 
:.
If you have further questions regarding this matter, please contact me at (916) 322-5660.
Sincerely,
Luisa Menchaca
General Counsel
,,\ 
!
,;,'-rl"{J{r{ Y.
By: TaraL. Stock
Political Reform Consultant
Technical Assistance Division

sections.question: The City of Sacramento requested approval to combine the city's post-election statement (due July 5, 2006, covering May 21, 2006 through June 30, 2006) and the semi-annual statement required by the Political Reform Act (due July 31, 2006, covering the same period) so that candidates and committees are not required to file two statements covering the same period.
sections.conclusion: Nothing in the Act prohibits a local jurisdiction from adopting its own rules imposing additional requirements provided those rules do not conflict with the Act. Since the city's post-election statement is due prior to the semi-annual statement filing deadline and includes all required expenditures and contributions, the semi-annual filing requirement is satisfied by the city's post-election statement filed by July 5, 2006. However, the Act's July 31, 2006 deadline for the semi-annual statement remains in effect, and a later filing would not violate the Act if accomplished by that deadline.
sections.question_synthetic: May a local jurisdiction combine its post-election campaign reporting requirement with the state-mandated semi-annual campaign statement filing requirement when both statements cover the same reporting period?
sections.conclusion_synthetic: A local jurisdiction may combine its post-election statement with the state semi-annual statement requirement, provided the combined filing occurs by the earlier deadline and includes all required information. The Act's July 31 deadline remains applicable, but filing by the earlier local deadline of July 5 satisfies both requirements.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Document is informal advice letter (marked as such in text). File number I-05-200 suggests informal designation. Standard QUESTION and CONCLUSION sections are not explicitly labeled but are clearly identifiable in the letter body. Footnote 2 notes that informal assistance does not provide immunity of formal opinion.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: The City of Sacramento requested approval to combine the city's post-election statement (due July 5, 2006, covering May 21, 2006 through June 30, 2006) and the semi-annual statement required by the Political Reform Act (due July 31, 2006, covering the same period) so that candidates and committees are not required to file two statements covering the same period.

CONCLUSION: Nothing in the Act prohibits a local jurisdiction from adopting its own rules imposing additional requirements provided those rules do not conflict with the Act. Since the city's post-election statement is due prior to the semi-annual statement filing deadline and includes all required expenditures and contributions, the semi-annual filing requirement is satisfied by the city's post-election statement filed by July 5, 2006. However, the Act's July 31, 2006 deadline for the semi-annual statement remains in effect, and a later filing would not violate the Act if accomplished by that deadline.

embedding.summary: The FPPC approved the City of Sacramento's request to combine its post-election campaign statement (due July 5, 2006) with the state-required semi-annual statement covering the same reporting period, provided the filing includes all required expenditures and contributions. The FPPC confirmed that while the combined filing satisfies both requirements when filed by July 5, the state's July 31 deadline for the semi-annual statement remains in effect under the Political Reform Act.
